# Glossary: Bioelectronic Medicine Architecture Terminology

## Terms A–M

**Active Implantable Medical Device (AIMD)** – Implantable device relying on a power source other than that generated by the body or gravity; the EU regulatory category (now under MDR) for such devices.

**AIROF (Activated Iridium Oxide Film)** – High charge-injection-capacity electrode coating material used in chronic neurostimulation electrodes.

**Blanking (artifact blanking)** – Circuit technique disconnecting/clamping a sensing amplifier during and briefly after a stimulation pulse to prevent saturation from the stimulation artifact.

**Charge balancing** – Ensuring net zero DC charge delivery per stimulation cycle via biphasic waveforms, preventing irreversible electrochemical tissue/electrode damage.

**Charge density** – Charge per phase divided by electrode surface area (μC/cm²); a primary safety-limiting parameter for stimulation electrode design.

**Chronaxie** – The pulse width corresponding to twice the rheobase current on a strength-duration curve; characterizes fiber-type-specific stimulation threshold behavior.

**Closed-loop stimulation** – Neuromodulation therapy that senses a physiological signal/biomarker and adjusts stimulation parameters responsively, as opposed to fixed/open-loop stimulation.

**Cuff electrode** – Electrode design wrapping circumferentially around a peripheral nerve, contacts facing inward toward the nerve surface.

**DBS (Deep Brain Stimulation)** – Neuromodulation targeting subcortical brain structures (e.g., subthalamic nucleus) for conditions like Parkinson's disease.

**ECAP (Evoked Compound Action Potential)** – Direct electrophysiological response of stimulated nerve fibers, sensed shortly after a stimulation pulse; used in some closed-loop SCS systems.

**Feedthrough** – Hermetically sealed electrical pass-through connecting internal implant electronics to external leads, typically using ceramic-metal or glass-metal seal technology.

**Gate control theory** – Proposed mechanism for SCS pain relief via large-fiber (Aβ) activation of inhibitory interneurons suppressing nociceptive signal transmission.

**Hermeticity** – Property of a package/enclosure being impermeable to moisture and gas ingress, essential for protecting implanted electronics.

**IDE (Investigational Device Exemption)** – FDA authorization allowing an investigational device to be used in a clinical study to collect safety/effectiveness data.

**Inductive wireless power transfer** – Power delivery to an implant via magnetic coupling between an external primary coil and an internal secondary coil.

**Inflammatory reflex** – Neurophysiological pathway by which vagal efferent signaling modulates systemic inflammation, the biological basis for bioelectronic anti-inflammatory VNS applications.

**IPG (Implantable Pulse Generator)** – The implanted device housing power, electronics, and control logic for a neurostimulation system.

**MICS (Medical Implant Communication Service)** – Regulated RF frequency band allocated for medical implant telemetry.

---

## Terms N–Z

**Paddle electrode** – Flat, multi-contact electrode array typically placed epidurally for spinal cord stimulation.

**Paresthesia** – Perceptible tingling sensation, historically a hallmark (and sometimes therapeutic target sensation) of conventional spinal cord stimulation.

**Penetrating electrode** – Electrode design inserted directly into neural tissue, offering higher selectivity at higher invasiveness than surface/cuff designs.

**PMA (Premarket Approval)** – Most rigorous FDA regulatory pathway, required for most active implantable neurostimulators (Class III devices).

**Rheobase** – The minimum current amplitude (at infinite pulse duration) required to elicit a neural response, per the strength-duration curve.

**SAR (Specific Absorption Rate)** – Measure of RF/electromagnetic energy absorption rate in tissue, a key safety metric for wireless power transfer and telemetry.

**SCS (Spinal Cord Stimulation)** – Neuromodulation targeting the dorsal columns of the spinal cord, primarily for chronic pain.

**Sham stimulation** – Non-therapeutic or sub-threshold stimulation used as a control condition in blinded neurostimulation clinical trials.

**Strength-duration curve** – Relationship between stimulation current amplitude and pulse width required to reach neural activation threshold.

**VNS (Vagus Nerve Stimulation)** – Neuromodulation targeting the vagus nerve, approved for epilepsy and depression, and under investigation for inflammatory/autonomic indications.

---

## Abbreviations Reference

| Abbr | Full Form |
|------|-----------|
| AIMD | Active Implantable Medical Device |
| AIROF | Activated Iridium Oxide Film |
| BMS | Battery Management System |
| DBS | Deep Brain Stimulation |
| ECAP | Evoked Compound Action Potential |
| EFS | Early Feasibility Study |
| IDE | Investigational Device Exemption |
| IEC 62304 | Medical device software lifecycle standard |
| IPG | Implantable Pulse Generator |
| ISO 10993 | Biological evaluation of medical devices standard |
| ISO 14708 | Active implantable medical devices standard series |
| MDR | EU Medical Device Regulation |
| MICS | Medical Implant Communication Service |
| PEDOT | Poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene), a conducting polymer |
| PMA | Premarket Approval |
| Pt-Ir | Platinum-Iridium |
| SAR | Specific Absorption Rate |
| SCS | Spinal Cord Stimulation |
| TiN | Titanium Nitride |
| VNS | Vagus Nerve Stimulation |

---

**Note:** This glossary is not exhaustive. Refer to topic files and primary sources (ISO, IEC, FDA, peer-reviewed neuromodulation literature) for authoritative definitions.
